title: Build failure on osx
body:
As per the README

```
brew install gpgme
git clone https://github.com/projectatomic/skopeo $GOPATH/src/github.com/projectatomic/skopeo
cd $GOPATH/src/github.com/projectatomic/skopeo && make binary-local
```

Output:

```
go build -ldflags "-X main.gitCommit=91606d49f2f7d17c226940e98b5f42500a4c3efb" -gcflags "" -tags "btrfs_noversion libdm_no_deferred_remove" -o skopeo ./cmd/skopeo
# pkg-config --cflags ostree-1 ostree-1 ostree-1 ostree-1 ostree-1 ostree-1
Package ostree-1 was not found in the pkg-config search path.
Perhaps you should add the directory containing `ostree-1.pc'
to the PKG_CONFIG_PATH environment variable
No package 'ostree-1' found
Package ostree-1 was not found in the pkg-config search path.
Perhaps you should add the directory containing `ostree-1.pc'
to the PKG_CONFIG_PATH environment variable
No package 'ostree-1' found
Package ostree-1 was not found in the pkg-config search path.
Perhaps you should add the directory containing `ostree-1.pc'
to the PKG_CONFIG_PATH environment variable
No package 'ostree-1' found
Package ostree-1 was not found in the pkg-config search path.
Perhaps you should add the directory containing `ostree-1.pc'
to the PKG_CONFIG_PATH environment variable
No package 'ostree-1' found
Package ostree-1 was not found in the pkg-config search path.
Perhaps you should add the directory containing `ostree-1.pc'
to the PKG_CONFIG_PATH environment variable
No package 'ostree-1' found
Package ostree-1 was not found in the pkg-config search path.
Perhaps you should add the directory containing `ostree-1.pc'
to the PKG_CONFIG_PATH environment variable
No package 'ostree-1' found
pkg-config: exit status 1
make: *** [binary-local] Error 2
```

comments.body: Looks like we need  a way to compile out the ostree support in containers/image for platforms that do not support it.

Thanks for your report.

Could you test and report back whether
```sh
make binary-local BUILDTAGS=containers_image_ostree_stub
```
works, please? If so, we can at least update the README, or a tested PR automating this for macOS would be also welcome.

(I can’t find a brew recipe for libostree, and with https://github.com/ostreedev/ostree/blob/master/src/libostree/ostree-linuxfsutil.c it seems unlikely that it would easily build for macOS, so compiling it out seems to be the right thing to do.)
Should we just add an ostree tag, and then only build ostree if this is specified?  Then we can get all of our Linux packages to specify ostree?

```
make binary-local BUILDTAGS=containers_image_ostree_stub
```

does indeed work

Great, thanks for testing. #381 is a minimal README update; autodetecting macOS in `Makefile` and adding the tag to `LOCAL_BUILD_TAGS` would be nice but I’ll leave that up to someone with a macOS development environment set up.
